title: MD
biography: Santiago Ramon y Cajal was a Spanish neuroscientist who established the neuron doctrine, proving that the nervous system is made of individual cells (neurons) rather than a continuous network. He shared the Nobel Prize in 1906 and is considered the father of modern neuroscience.
aiSummary: Santiago Ramon y Cajal was the father of modern neuroscience who established the neuron doctrine. Nobel Prize 1906 for work on the structure of the nervous system. Based in Madrid, Spain.
techniquesInvented: Golgi staining modifications for neuronal visualization

affiliations.role: Professor of Histology and Pathological Anatomy

awards: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1906)

timeline.title: Published neuron doctrine evidence
timeline.description: Using modified Golgi staining, demonstrated neurons are discrete cells with distinct connections

timeline.title: Les Nouvelles Idees sur la Structure du Systeme Nerveux
timeline.description: Published comprehensive work establishing the neuron as the fundamental unit of the nervous system

timeline.title: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
timeline.description: Shared with Camillo Golgi for work on the structure of the nervous system
